title: Five EU countries 'deliberately eroding' rule of law, report shows
description: A new report by the Civil Liberties Union of Europe points to “deliberate erosion of the rule of law” in Bulgaria, Croatia, Hungary, Italy, and Slovakia. FRANCE 24's Brussels correspondent Dave Keating reports.

title: No, Iranian hackers didn’t leak a video of the FBI's Kash Patel dancing
description: FBI Director Kash Patel has been the subject of social media buzz this week, after his private emails were hacked by an Iranian-linked group. There's also a viral Bollywood dance video being falsely linked to the incident, despite no connection to Patel, as Vedika Bahl clears up in Truth or Fake.

title: Report finds there are no safe places for women in Darfur
description: In tonight's edition, Doctors Without Borders sounds the alarm over Sudan, warning sexual violence against women and girls is being used as a weapon of war. Also, a Tunisian ⁠court handed down a ​two-year prison sentence to news website editor Ghassen Ben Khelifa in ​the latest prosecution targeting media workers. And after stops in New York, London and Melbourne, the Africa Fashion exhibition arrives in Paris.

title: US journalist kidnapped in Baghdad by suspected Iranian-backed armed group
description: A woman US journalist who was kidnapped in Iraq's capital city by a suspected Iranian-backed Iraqi armed group, US officials said on Tuesday. Advocacy groups identified the missing woman as Shelly Kittleson, a freelance journalist based in Rome who has covered several wars in the Middle East..

title: Eurovision Song Contest to hold first Asian edition in Bangkok in November
description: The Eurovision Song Contest Asia 2026 has confirmed artists from at least 10 countries across Asia competing: Thailand, South Korea, the Philippines, Vietnam, Malaysia, Cambodia, Laos, Bangladesh, Nepal and Bhutan. More are expected to join before the finale in November. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis reports.

title: Analysis: The Islamic State’s targeting of a Chinese mine in Congo proves the group is a resilient threat
description:
The Islamic State’s Central Africa Province (ISCAP), known locally as the Allied Democratic Forces (ADF), raided and burned down a Chinese-owned mine in eastern Congo earlier this month. The raid, which was far from the jihadist group’s normal area of operation, highlights both ISCAP’s threats to Congo’s mining sector and its resilience after nearly five years of a sustained military operation against it.

title: Interim Venezuelan president ousts defense chief as Washington makes limited progress in Caracas
description: On March 18, Venezuelan acting President Delcy Rodríguez removed longtime Defense Minister Vladimir Padrino López and installed Gustavo González López, followed by initiating a broad restructuring of the military’s top leadership. These moves unfolded alongside US-supported stabilization efforts and the passage of a February 20 amnesty law that has facilitated the release of dozens of political prisoners. Dr. Escurignan explores the cultural, economic, and deeply personal dynamics surrounding Celine Dion’s highly anticipated return to the global stage. title: Hungarian FM says reports he passed information to Russia are 'foreign interference'
description: Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto said on Tuesday that reports from Eastern European media outlets that he passed "strategic information" to Russia were "foreign interference". In one leaked phone call Szijjarto is alleged to have told Russian counterpart Sergei Lavrov that he was "at his service".

title: Gulf allies privately make the case to Trump to keep fighting until Iran is decisively defeated
description: Gulf states, led by Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, are urging Trump to continue prosecuting the war against Iran, arguing that Tehran hasn't been weakened enough by the bombing campaign, according to U.S., Gulf and Israeli officials. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Dr H. A. Hellyer, Senior Fellow at the Royal United Services Institute. title: Burundi's main city rocked by blasts after electrical fire at military store
description: Multiple blasts hit Burundi's main city of Bujumbura on Tuesday after an electrical fire broke out at a military arsenal in the east African nation. An army spokesperson said emergency services were intervening and urged the public to remain calm after the incident sparked fears of a coup against the government.

title: US Supreme Court rules against ban on 'conversion therapy' for LGBTQ minors
description: The US Supreme Court ruled Tuesday against a Colorado state law banning "conversion therapy" for LGBTQ minors, siding with a Christian therapist who challenged it on the grounds of free speech. At issue is the constitutionality of a 2019 Colorado law that prohibits licensed practitioners from conducting "conversion therapy" on patients under 18. The Guardian Washington DC bureau chief David Smith reports.

title: Europe pushes back on US military operations as concerns over Iran war mount
description: France and Italy have joined Spain in pushing back on U.S.-Israeli military operations, sources said on Tuesday, as U.S. President Donald Trump singled out NATO allies in Europe as unhelpful in the month-long war, highlighting divisions. title: Can Lebanon keep it together? Government faces Israeli invasion, standoff with Hezbollah
description: The Lebanese have seen it all, from civil war to the 2019 protests demanding an end to revolving door politics, from the 2020 port of Beirut explosion that exposed the tragic consequences of nepotism to Israel’s 2024 operation to neutralize longtime foe Hezbollah. Now Israel’s ground forces are back to again take on Tehran-backed foe Hezbollah. Thus, Lebanon finds itself the second front of the month-long war launched against Iran.

title: EU shows support to Ukraine at Bucha commemoration
description: Four years on from the Bucha massacre, the Ukrainian town is remembering its dead. Russian military forces retreated from Bucha in 2022, hundreds of bodies were discovered, including women, children and elderly people, many of the corpses had been left lying in the streets. A delegation of EU foreign ministers arrived in Kyiv on March 31 to mark the anniversary and discuss ways to further support Ukraine.

title: As Ukraine commemorates the Bucha massacre, Russia rejects idea of Easter truce
description: Ukraine is commemorating the fourth anniversary of the Bucha massacre, named after a town who many of its residents are thought to have been massacred by Russian invading troops in the early weeks of the war. Since, the conflict hasn't slowed down, as Russia keeps pounding the country. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle tells us more.

title: Israel to occupy swaths of south Lebanon and destroy houses near border
description: Israel's defence minister has vowed his army will block residents from returning to southern Lebanon after the war and will be destroying all homes in villages along the border, in which he implied would be a Gaza-like scale of destruction as seen in Rafah and Beit Hanoun. More than 1200 people have been killed in Lebanon and over 1.2 million displaced since Israel began its offensive against Hezbollah at the start of March.

Our reporters get an insight into this showcase of continental style, from the ancestral weaves of traditional dress to the African creations making a splash on contemporary red carpets.

title: Iran desalination plant in the Strait of Hormuz out of service following strikes
description: One of the desalination plants on Iran's Qeshm Island has been out ​of ‌service since being ⁠hit by an air strike earlier ‌in the month, an official from ⁠the Health Ministry told Iranian media on March 30. title: How Trump’s dream of seizing enriched uranium from Iran could end in catastrophe

title: Easter eggs smaller and more expensive as cocoa prices remain high
description: Inflation, war in the middle east, energy prices - consumers really haven't been having a great time of it lately, and we're sorry to report that Easter and its habitual chocolates aren't really going to relieve the gloom either. It turns out not only have the world's beloved easter eggs been getting smaller - they're getting pricer as well - and this despite a recent crash in cocoa prices.

'This law will have an impact on what little is left of Israel's diplomatic standing', says FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky from Jerusalem.

title: EU ministers in Kyiv to look at justice for crimes committed in Bucha
description: EU diplomats are travelling to Ukraine to mark the fourth anniversary of the massacre in the city of Bucha. It occurred only weeks into the war: after Russia evacuate the town, returnees found hundreds of bodies of men, women, children and elderly people who had been mass executed. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert looks back on an event that became a symbol of what many towns in Ukraine have experienced since the start of the war.

title: Middle East war impacts world tourism as travelers consider trip choices

title: With Iran, US 'trying to put the best face on a mission they did not anticipate'

FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert looks at the takeaways from the press conference, which seemed to be yet another attempt at 'trying to put the best face on a mission' that lacked preparation.

title: 'Cultural heritage belongs to all of humanity': Iranian archaeologist Sepideh Maziar

Sepideh Maziar is a senior researcher and lecturer at Goethe University Frankfurt and a founding member of the Society for Iranian Archaeology. Kharg Island looks like the perfect target — take it, and you choke off Iran’s oil. But it’s not that simple. Bill Roggio is joined by Ryan Brobst and Cameron McMillan to discuss why seizing Kharg could hand Tehran exactly what it wants: a wider war, a vulnerable US position, and a fight on the regime’s terms.

While attention has shifted to the conflicts in Iran and Lebanon, Gaza remains under a fragile ceasefire, with ongoing low-level clashes. High Representative for Gaza Nickolay Mladenov highlighted progress toward a disarmament framework tied to UN-backed plans. Despite this, Hamas continues to consolidate control in parts of the territory as Israel targets militants and terrorist infrastructure.

description:
Turkish spy chief Ibrahim Kalin’s March 22 meeting with senior Hamas leaders in Istanbul highlights Turkey’s continued high-level engagement with the group, reinforcing Ankara’s role as a mediator in Gaza ceasefire negotiations while omitting any discussion of Hamas’s disarmament. The meeting reflects a broader pattern of Turkish support for the group, evidenced by recent US Treasury sanctions on Turkish charities linked to Hamas.

The visit is the first to Germany for Syria's interim leader, with critics saying his human rights record is being ignored. It is also the newest attempt by al-Sharaa to kindle a relationship with Western countries, after years of isolation for Syria under dictator Bashar al-Assad. FRANCE 24's Rochelle Ferguso

title: Three UN peacekeepers in Lebanon killed in 24 hours
description: In Lebanon, two Indonesian peacekeepers were killed in the south of the country on March 30 in an explosion. The UN agency tasked with policing the region said "an explosion of unknown origin destroyed their vehicle." It comes just a day after another Indonesian peacekeeper was killed when a projectile of unknown origin struck southern Lebanon, and amid ongoing exchanges of fire between Israel and Hezbollah.

title: Crude prices whipsaw on mixed signals from Iran war
description: Oil prices searched for direction on Tuesday amid conflicting events related to the Iran war. While US President Donald Trump's threats that the US would obliterate Iranian energy infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz remained closed led to an increase in prices, a later report saying Trump is seeking an end to the war brought them down. Also in this edition: inflation in France ticks up because of higher energy prices and Australia threatens le

title: Ukraine's Bucha commemorates four years since massacre
description: Four years on from the Bucha massacre, the Ukrainian town is remembering its dead. When Russian military forces evacuated the city in 2022, hundreds of bodies of men, women, children and elderly people were discovered, with many lying in the street. A delegation of EU ministers is set to arrive to mark the anniversary and discuss ways to further support Ukraine.

title: Gaza's evacuated premature babies return to devastated strip
description:
Eleven children born prematurely, who were later evacuated to Egypt during the war, have returned to Gaza.
 
The children were moved in the first weeks of the conflict when the hospital where they were being cared for was besieged by the Israeli military. The return to their families has been marked by uncertainty in a Gaza Strip devastated by two and a half years of war.

title: Spain closes airspace to US planes involved in attacks on Iran
description: Spain's airspace is now closed to U.S. planes involved in attacks on Iran, a step beyond its previous denial of use of jointly-operated military bases. Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has become one of the most vocal critics of the US and Israeli attacks in Iran, describing them as reckless and illegal.

title: EU diplomats mark Bucha massacre anniversary, affirms support for Ukraine
description: European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas and foreign ministers of several member states marked the fourth anniversary of the Bucha massacre at a commemoration ceremony in the Kyiv suburb on Tuesday. The ceremony comes amid tensions within the EU over Hungary blocking an aid package for Ukraine.

description: U.S. average gas prices have hit $4 per gallon nationwide, per AAA.Why it matters: It's a 35% increase at the pump since the war in Iran began last month, and there's little hope that costs will ease as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed to most ships, straining supplies worldwide.Approximately 20% of the world's oil supply travels through the Strait.Driving the news: Oil prices have surged to over $100 a barrel or more since the war began, rais

title: Trump pursues era of unshackled warfare
description: President Trump's threat to bomb Iran's water supply would constitute his most dramatic breach of the laws and norms designed to protect civilians in wartime.Why it matters: The Iran war is the biggest test of what Trump's contempt for "politically correct" war-fighting looks like in practice.His administration has already signed off on Israeli assassinations of political leaders, threatened "no quarter" for enemy combatants, and initially reject

title: Coalition to advance ultra-Orthodox draft exemption bill after pause due to Iran war
description:
Foreign Affairs and Defense Committee chair Bismuth says work will resume on the controversial legislation when war ends, after IDF chief warned cabinet that manpower shortages were worsening

title: Four soldiers killed, 2 wounded in south Lebanon clash with Hezbollah
description:
Slain troops named as Cpt. Noam Madmoni, Staff Sgt. Ben Cohen, Staff Sgt. Maxsim Entis, Staff Sgt. Gilad Harel; IDF says it captured Hezbollah operative surveilling troops

title: Eight lightly hurt, damage reported as Iran fires missile salvos at central Israel
description:
Four soldiers killed in south Lebanon clash with Hezbollah * Trump said to tell aides he’s willing to end war without reopening Hormuz * PM: I won’t put timeline on when war will end

title: Middle East war live: Iran attacks tanker at Dubai Port, Chinese vessels transit Strait of Hormuz
description: An Iranian attack on a Kuwait-flagged oil tanker at Dubai Port on Tuesday caused a fire, but no injuries or oil spill, according to Emirati authorities. Meanwhile China on Tuesday confirmed that three Chinese ships were allowed to transit the Strait of Hormuz in recent days. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

title: Somali army takes over key city in Southwest as state leader resigns
description: Somali federal government hails Baidoas transition, urging peace and unity amid political changes and rising tensions.

title: Iran war wipes $120bn off Dubai, Abu Dhabi stock markets
description: The UAEs financial markets have been some of the hardest hit by the fallout of the US-Israel war on Iran.

title: Iran war: What is happening on day 32 of US-Israel attacks?
description: In an exclusive interview with Al Jazeera, Secretary of State Rubio said US war objectives would be achieved in weeks.

title: US says Cuba policy unchanged despite allowing Russian tanker
description: The White House says US policy toward Cuba remains unchanged despite allowing a sanctioned Russian tanker.

title: Palestine weekly wrap: Holy sites remain closed as deadly violence spreads
description: Settler attacks, East Jerusalem evictions, shuttering of sacred sites, as Israeli authorities assert control.

title: Video: Newborns evacuated from Gaza return as toddlers over two years later
description: A group of toddlers that evacuated from Gaza in 2023 have returned to their families in emotional reuniting.

title: Trump Faces a Decision on Whether to Start a Ground War in Iran
description: The president wants a negotiation, but the Iranians say they are refusing until a cease-fire is declared. And while Marines and the 82nd Airborne Division offer new leverage, the risks escalate quickly.

title: Lawsuit Accuses Americans of Political Killings for Hire Overseas
description: Three men working for an American security contractor say the United Arab Emirates paid them to carry out targeted killings in Yemen. One of their targets is suing them in U.S. federal court.

title: В Пакистане невеста открыла стрельбу на свадьбе и сбежала вместе с супругом
description: В Пакистане невеста устроила стрельбу в ходе свадебной церемонии. Об этом сообщает издание Pakistan Today.

title: В Ливане погибли четверо израильских военных
description: На юге Ливана в столкновениях с боевиками "Хезболлы" погибли четыре военнослужащих ВС Израиля, еще двое были ранены, пишет The Times of Israel со ссылкой на пресс-службу Армии обороны Израиля.

title: After backing Iran with Israel strike, Houthis avoid further escalation, for now
description:

title: Trump threatens to "obliterate" Iran's energy and water infrastructure
description: President Trump threatened on Monday that if a deal with Iran "is not shortly reached" and if the Hormuz Strait isn't open "immediately," the U.S. will "completely obliterate" Iran's power, energy and water infrastructure. Why it matters: Destroying those facilities would not only have devastating implications for Iranian civilians, it could also deepen the war-driven global energy crisis.
